title: Quiz: Descriptor-Based Catalyst Design
description: Test your understanding of catalyst descriptors and screening

questions.question: In the scaling relation E(AHₓ) = γ × E(A) + ξ, what determines the slope γ?

options.text: The ratio of remaining to maximum bonds
The d-band center of the metal

questions.explanation: The slope γ = (v_max - x)/v_max, where v_max is the maximum valence of atom A and x is the number of H atoms. This reflects how the bonding capacity is distributed between metal-A and A-H bonds.

questions.question: On a volcano plot, what characterizes the left (weak binding) side?
questions.explanation: The left side of a volcano plot corresponds to weak binding. Here, the catalyst cannot effectively activate reactants because they don't bind strongly enough. This is the activation-limited regime.

questions.question: Why is the universal OOH-OH scaling relation problematic for ORR catalyst design?

options.text: It limits how close any catalyst can get to optimal binding
It only applies to noble metals

questions.explanation: The fixed ~3.2 eV difference between E(OOH) and E(OH) means catalysts cannot independently optimize both intermediates. This sets a fundamental limit on ORR activity, explaining why Pt is hard to improve upon.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Gaussian Process models in catalyst screening?

options.text: They are the fastest ML models
They provide uncertainty estimates with predictions
They only work with electronic descriptors

questions.explanation: Gaussian Process models provide both predictions and uncertainty estimates. This is crucial for active learning, where you want to prioritize calculating materials where the model is uncertain or predicts high activity.

questions.question: Single-atom catalysts can break scaling relations because their coordination environment differs from bulk surfaces.
questions.explanation: True. Single-atom catalysts (SACs) like M-N₄ sites have unique coordination environments that don't follow the same scaling relations as bulk metal surfaces. This allows them to potentially circumvent the limitations imposed by conventional scaling.

questions.question: Which effects can alloying introduce to modify catalyst reactivity? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Alloying introduces three main effects: ligand (electronic modification from neighboring atoms), strain (d-band shift from lattice expansion/compression), and ensemble (different site geometries). Spin-orbit coupling is a separate relativistic effect.
